title: The Distributive Property

projects.title: Unit 6 Project — Expressions in Action
projects.desc: Capstone: apply exponents, expressions, and the distributive property to a real-world project.

projects.title: Distributive Alchemy
projects.desc: Use the distributive property to transform and expand expressions.

projects.title: Expression Engine
projects.desc: Build, evaluate, and match algebraic expressions in an interactive game.

contentObjective: I can use the distributive property to expand and factor expressions.
languageObjective: I can explain my steps using the words distributive property, factor, expand, and equivalent.

turnAndTalk.question: Each bundle is a ticket ($15) and a snack ($5). For 3 people, why does 3(15 + 5) give the same $60 as 3 × 15 + 3 × 5?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The distributive property multiplies the 3 by each item, giving the same total.

stems.en: Both give $60 because I distribute the 3 to ___ and ___.
stems.es: Las dos dan $60 porque distribuyo el 3 a ___ y ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain the 3 distributes to both the ticket and the snack (3 × 15 + 3 × 5 = 45 + 15 = 60), matching 3 × 20, and use the word equivalent.
turnAndTalk.extend: If 10 people bought bundles, which form is faster to compute: 10(15 + 5) or 10 × 15 + 10 × 5? Justify your choice.
extendStems: Either way the total is ___, since both forms are equivalent because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: When you expand 5(2x + 4), why must the 5 multiply BOTH terms inside the parentheses?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The factor outside multiplies every term inside the parentheses.

stems.en: I distribute the 5 to ___ and ___, so 5(2x + 4) = ___.

stems.es: Los dos términos se multiplican porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: A strong answer expands 5(2x + 4) = 10x + 20 and explains the parentheses mean 5 copies of the whole sum, so both 2x and 4 are multiplied.
turnAndTalk.extend: A classmate writes 5(2x + 4) = 10x + 4. Critique the error and prove the correct form is equivalent by testing x = 3.
extendStems: The error is ___ because they only multiplied ___.
Testing x = 3: 5(2·3 + 4) = ___ but 10·3 + 4 = ___, so they are not equivalent.

turnAndTalk.question: For 8 practice rooms each needing a music stand ($12) and a stool ($9), the cost is 8(12 + 9). How do you use the distributive property to find the total, and which form is easier for you?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Distribute the 8 to both items, then add.

stems.en: I think the ___ form is easier because ___.
stems.es: Creo que la forma ___ es más fácil porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute 8 × 12 + 8 × 9 = 96 + 72 = 168 (or 8 × 21 = 168) and justify which form they find easier, recognizing both are equivalent.
turnAndTalk.extend: Working backwards, how would you factor 8x + 12 into a product? Explain how factoring undoes the distributive property.

turnAndTalk.question: During practice on The Distributive Property, what strategy did you use when a problem felt tricky?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The number outside the parentheses gets "shared" with every term inside.

stems.en: I distribute the 5 to ___ and ___, so 5(2x + 4) = ___.
I knew my answer was reasonable because ___.
stems.es: Supe que mi respuesta era razonable porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Listen for students naming a specific strategy tied to 6.EE.3 — not just "I multiplied." They should connect steps to the key idea.
turnAndTalk.extend: How would you help a classmate who made the common mistake from today's lesson? Use one vocabulary word.
extendStems: I would tell them to ___ first because ___.
The mistake happens when you ___ instead of ___.

vocabulary.definition: Multiplying a number by everything inside the parentheses: a(b + c) = ab + ac.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Multiplicar un número por todo lo que está dentro del paréntesis: a(b + c) = ab + ac.
vocabulary.visual: 3(4 + 5) = 3×4 + 3×5 = 12 + 15 = 27 — the 3 gets 'distributed' to both the 4 and the 5
vocabulary.cloze: The rule that a(b + c) = ab + ac is the ___.

examples.why: The 3 is multiplied by each term inside.

vocabulary.definition: A number that gets multiplied by another number.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un número que se multiplica por otro número.
vocabulary.visual: In 3(x + 2), the 3 is the factor outside the parentheses that multiplies each term inside
vocabulary.cloze: To rewrite a sum as a product, like 3x + 12 = 3(x + 4), is to ___.

vocabulary.definition: To multiply out the parentheses in an expression.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Multiplicar lo que está dentro del paréntesis en una expresión.
vocabulary.cloze: To multiply out a product over a sum, like 3(x + 4) = 3x + 12, is to ___.

vocabulary.definition: Expressions that always have the same value.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Expresiones que siempre tienen el mismo valor.
vocabulary.visual: 3(x + 4) and 3x + 12 always give the same answer: when x = 2, both = 18; when x = 10, both = 42
vocabulary.cloze: Two expressions that always have the same value are ___.

vocabulary.definition: The number in front of a letter, like the 3 in 3x.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El número frente a una letra, como el 3 en 3x.
vocabulary.visual: In 6x + 15, the coefficient of x is 6 — it came from distributing in 3(2x + 5)
vocabulary.cloze: A number multiplied by a variable is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: Terms with the same letter, like 2x and 5x.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Términos con la misma letra, como 2x y 5x.
vocabulary.cloze: Terms with the same variable part are ___.

launch.narrative: You're selling concert bundles at the music studio. Each bundle includes a ticket ($15) and a snack ($5). If 3 people each buy a bundle, you can calculate the total as 3(15 + 5) OR as 3 × 15 + 3 × 5. Either way, the total is the same — $60! This is the distributive property.
launch.contextImage: Music studio ticket booth showing concert bundles with tickets and snacks
noticePrompts: Why do both methods give the same total?
wonderPrompts: What if 10 people bought bundles — which way would be faster to calculate?
Does this work with subtraction too, like 4(20 − 3)?

conceptIntro.heading: What is the distributive property?
conceptIntro.intro: The distributive property means you can multiply a number by everything inside the parentheses, one piece at a time: a(b + c) = ab + ac.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: The number outside the parentheses gets "shared" with every term inside.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: Each bundle has a ticket ($15) and a snack ($5). For 3 bundles I can write 3(15 + 5).
Way 2: distribute the 3 to each part. 3 × 15 + 3 × 5.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: Let's expand 2(8 + 3). Where does the 2 go?
We multiply the 2 by each term: 2 × 8 + 2 × 3.

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: In the table, expand each expression using the distributive property, then simplify.

explore.instructions: Expand each expression using the distributive property. Then simplify to find the value.

discourse.prompt: What pattern do you see when you expand an expression using the distributive property?
discourse.sentenceFrame: When I distribute, I multiply the outside factor ___ by each term ___. The operation inside the parentheses ___ stays the same.

optional.stem: Light Cue 1: Expand 3(x + 4) for the stage-light controller.
optional.explanation: Distribute the 3 to each term: 3 × x + 3 × 4 = 3x + 12.

optional.stem: Light Cue 2: Expand 5(2 + n) to set two spotlight banks.
optional.explanation: Distribute the 5 to each term: 5 × 2 + 5 × n = 10 + 5n.

optional.stem: Light Cue 3: Expand 4(a + 2) for the back-row beam.
optional.explanation: Distribute the 4 to each term: 4 × a + 4 × 2 = 4a + 8.

optional.label: Match each a(b + c) light cue to its ab + ac expanded form.

optional.instructions: Sort each light-cue expansion: was the factor distributed correctly to BOTH terms, or only to one?

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — The Spotlight Only Hit One Term
optional.correctWork: 3(x + 4) = 3 × x + 3 × 4 = 3x + 12. The factor 3 is like a spotlight that must shine on EVERY term inside the parentheses, not just the first one. Picture the area model: a 3-by-x panel (3x) plus a 3-by-4 panel (12).
hints: Did the 3 reach both terms inside the parentheses, or only the x?
The distributive property says a(b + c) = ab + ac — both b and c get multiplied.

approaching.stem: When using the distributive property with a(b + c), you must multiply 'a' by:
approaching.explanation: The distributive property says a(b + c) = ab + ac. You multiply the outside factor by EACH term inside the parentheses.

approaching.instructions: Use the distributive property to expand each expression.

approaching.title: approaching — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on The Distributive Property.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: The number outside the parentheses gets "shared" with every term inside.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

approaching.correctWork: Follow the key idea: The number outside the parentheses gets "shared" with every term inside.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: The number outside the parentheses gets "shared" with every term inside.

onLevel.instructions: Use the distributive property to expand each expression.

onLevel.label: Match each factored form to its expanded form.

onLevel.instructions: Expand each expression, then sort by whether the expanded form has a constant greater than 15 or not.
Are these expressions equivalent? Left: 4(x + 3). Right: 4x + 12. Test with x = 5.
onLevel.label: Both equal 32 when x = 5 — they are equivalent by the distributive property.

onLevel.title: onLevel — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on The Distributive Property.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: The number outside the parentheses gets "shared" with every term inside.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

onLevel.correctWork: Follow the key idea: The number outside the parentheses gets "shared" with every term inside.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: The number outside the parentheses gets "shared" with every term inside.

extending.title: Find the Distributive Error
extending.correctWork: 5(2x + 4) = 5 × 2x + 5 × 4 = 10x + 20. You must distribute the 5 to BOTH terms inside the parentheses, not just the first one.
hints: Did the student multiply 5 by both terms, or only the first term?
The distributive property says a(b + c) = ab + ac. Both b and c get multiplied.

extending.prompt: Use the distributive property to explain why 6 × 98 can be calculated as 6 × 100 − 6 × 2 = 588. Then create your own example of using the distributive property to make multiplication easier.

extending.instructions: Work backwards: factor each expanded expression into its factored form using the GCF.

optionalActivity.name: Distributive Property Stage Lights
optionalActivity.intro: Program the studio's stage lights by distributing each factor to every term in the cue.

practice.commonMistake: A common mistake in The Distributive Property is skipping the key idea: "The number outside the parentheses gets "shared" with every term inside." — always check your work against this rule before you submit.

connect.scenario: A music studio is ordering supplies for 8 practice rooms. Each room needs a music stand ($12) and a stool ($9). The total cost can be written as 8(12 + 9).
connect.promptQuestion: Use the distributive property to find the total cost. Which way of calculating is easier for you?
connect.prompt: 8(12 + 9) = 8 × ___ + 8 × ___ = ___ + ___ = ___. I think the ___ form is easier because ___.
